title: Optimization and validation of a microвҖ“QuEChERS method for phthalates detection in small samples of cetacean blubberвң°
author: Sambolino, Rodriguez, et al.
description: In this study, an innovative method was developed to detect and quantify phthalates in fresh cetacean blubber. An adaptation of the ammonium formate QuEChERS method was used and adapted as a micro-extraction for small quantities of samples. Significantly, this technique utilized minimal quantities of reagents and salts, with the additional implementation of rigorous Quality Assurance/Quality Control protocols to further reduce background contamination. To ensure the reliability of this method, comprehensive validation procedures were conducted, with a specific focus on two widely studied cetacean species: the common bottlenose dolphin (Tursiops truncatus) and the short-finned pilot whale (Globicephala macrorhynchus). Determination coefficients (R2) for matrix-matched calibration were >0.93 with limits of quantifications (LOQ) of the method in the range of 5вҖ“10В ng/g. Mean recovery values were between 40 and 100В %. This novel methodology holds particular relevance for environmental research studies, offering the capability to detect emerging contaminants with minimal sample requirements. This aspect is particularly valuable in investigations that involve free-ranging animals and rely on biopsy sampling. It allows for the assessment of contaminant levels in healthy individuals within wild populations, enhancing our understanding of ecological impacts and potential conservation measures.вҖўA micro-extraction adaptation of the ammonium formate QuEChERS method was developed and applied to a small quantity of fresh cetacean blubber to detect phthalates.вҖўSmall quantities of reagents and salts were used, and additional Quality Assurance/ Quality Control procedures were taken to further minimize background contamination.вҖўMethod validation was carried out for two cosmopolitan and extensively studied cetacean species: the common bottlenose dolphin (Tursiops truncatus) and the short-finned pilot whale (Globicephala macrorhynchus).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: A modified version of the QuEChERS method described in Sambolino etВ al. [1] was applied to a small quantity of cetacean blubber for phthalates extraction and purification (named micro- QuEChERS). The present method, in order to be applied in 100x smaller quantity of tissue, sees a considerably reduced amount of extraction reagents, while maintaining the same amount of purification salts, given the higher fat concentrations of the target tissue. An extra step was introduced to concentrate the final extract, given the limited amount of analytes extractable from such small samples. For this reason, additional cleaning procedures were implemented to control background contamination, minimizing the risk of co-extraction of phthalates in reagents and glassware. The target analytes in this modified approach are seven phthalates which are the most commonly encountered in the marine environment [2], [3], [4].

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Blubber tissue was collected from stranded cetaceans (common bottlenose dolphin - Tursiops truncatus and short-finned pilot whale - Globicephala macrorhynchus) and kept frozen at вҲ’20В° C until the day of analysis. On the analysis day, a small portion of sample (50В mg, wet weight [w.w.]) was cut, added to a 15В mL glass tube, and mashed, still frozen, with a glass rod for at least 1В min. One ml of Acetonitrile (ACN) was then added to the glass tube with the freeze-homogenized sample, vortexed for 1В min, then 0.5В g of ammonium formate was added, in order to induce phase separation [5]; the mixture was vortexed again for 1В min and then centrifuged for 5В min at 2500В rpm. For the purification step, all the supernatant was transferred to a glass tube containing 150В mg of MgSO4, 50В mg of Primary Secondary Amine (PSA), and 50В mg of C18. The ratio of ammonium formate to ACN and MgSO4 in PSA and C18 followed established methodologies [1,5], although quantities of reagents were considerably reduced in comparison to the original method [1]. The mixture was again vortexed for 1В min and centrifuged for 5В min at 2500В rpm. The resulting supernatant (200В Вөl) was transferred to a GC vial, evaporated overnight, and then reconstituted in 50В Вөl of Cyclohexane (CH).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: To minimize contamination, all the glassware was carefully cleaned, immersed in an acid bath for 24В h, and then muffled at 550В° C overnight [1,6]. No plastic material besides pipette tips (phthalate-free) was used during the procedure. To minimize background contamination from organic reagents, ACN and CH were double distilled before use [7] and salts were washed three times with methanol. Two procedural blanks were analyzed with each batch of samples, and an average of the two blank values (RSD < 10В %) was subtracted from the final results. High-purity solvents and reagents were used. Methanol, ACN and CH of LC-MS grade, ammonium formate (purity вүҘ 97.0В %) and MgSO4 (purity вүҘ 98.0В %) were from VWR International Eurolab (Barcelona, Spain). PSA and C18 were from Agilent Technologies (Santa Clara, CA, USA).

steps_raw.title: Method details
steps_raw.instruction: Seven phthalates (Phthalic Acid Ester - PAE) were the target analytes (chemical structures and proprieties in TableВ 1). High purity standards (>98В %) of each PAE and two isotopically labeled PAEs (DEP-D4 and DBP-D4, used as internal standards) were acquired from Sigma-Aldrich (Madrid, Spain) and Dr. Ehrenstorfer (Augsburg, Germany). The determination and quantification of the analytes was carried out with an Agilent 6890 GC (Agilent Technologies, California, USA), equipped with an Ultra Inert HP-5В ms capillary column (30В mВ Г—В 0.25В mm inner diameter, 0.25В Вөm film thickness), coupled with an Agilent 5973 Network MS (Agilent Technologies, California, USA), in the selected ion monitoring (SIM) mode. The oven temperature program started at 60 В°C (1В min), then increased to 190 В°C at 45 В°C/min, and finally increased to 310 В°C at 35 В°C/min and held for 3В min. Injection was in splitless mode (purge time 0.75В min; purge flow 40В ml/min) at 280 В°C, with an injection volume of 2В Вөl. The detection parameters follow those described in Sambolino etВ al. steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: The modified QuEChERS method (micro-QuEChERS) was validated through several steps. First, an instrument calibration using the internal standard approach was done in order to quantify the blank values and calculate the matrix effect (TableВ 3). Recoveries studies at two spiking levels (25 and 150В ng/g w.w.) and matrix-matched calibrations with the internal standard method were then carried out for both matrices (blubber samples from the two species) (TablesВ 4 and 5). The equations coefficient obtained were used to quantify the compounds in the analyzed samples. The studied linear range, the matrix effect (ME) and the limits of quantifications (LOQ) of the method, considered as the lowest calibration level with signal to noise ratio > 10, are reported in TableВ 5 for the two different matrices analyzed.Table 3Internal instrumental calibration data of the target analytes.Table 3AnalyteStudied linear range (Вөg/L)Regression equation (nВ =В 8)sy/xR2b Вұ sbВ·t(0.05;7)a Вұ saВ·t(0.05;7)DMP5вҖ“300(5.37В ВұВ 0.49)В·10вҲ’3(0.54В ВұВ 6.7)В·10вҲ’24.67В·10вҲ’20.9938DEP5вҖ“300(7.68В ВұВ 0.72)В·10вҲ’3(7.64В ВұВ 9.84)В·10вҲ’26.86В·10вҲ’20.9934DIBP5вҖ“300(10.19В ВұВ 1.24)В·10вҲ’3(4.06В ВұВ 17.57)В·10вҲ’210.92В·10вҲ’20.9924DBP5вҖ“300(11.68В ВұВ 1.05)В·10вҲ’3(1.31В ВұВ 14.43)В·10вҲ’210.06В·10вҲ’20.9939BBP5вҖ“300(4.19В ВұВ 0.42)В·10вҲ’3(5.88В ВұВ 6.57)В·10вҲ’23.11В·10вҲ’20.9970DEHP5вҖ“300(6.44В ВұВ 0.73)В·10вҲ’3(3.22В ВұВ 10.65)В·10вҲ’25.39В·10вҲ’20.9962DNOP5вҖ“300(9.42В ВұВ 1.04)В·10вҲ’3(1.52В ВұВ 16.36)В·10вҲ’27.74В·10вҲ’20.9964b: slope; Sb: standard error of the slope; t(0.05;7): t-multiplier for 95В % confidence interval calculation; a: intercept; Sa: standard error of the intercept; sy/x: standard error of the estimate; R2: determination coefficient.Table 4Relative recovery (%) and RSD values (in brackets) of the target analytes from recovery studies with two spiking levels on blubber samples.Table 4Matrix (Species)AnalyteLevel 1Level 2MeanShort-finned pilot whale (Globicephala macrorhynchus)DMP88 (37)108 (43)100 (38)DEP61 (30)121 (41)97 (51)DIBP72 (5)103 (4)88 (20)DBP59 (6)74 (3)67 (13)BBP64 (5)41 (9)53 (24)DEHP70 (9)37 (10)53 (35)DNOP162 (13)23 (14)93 (83)Bottlenose dolphin (Tursiops truncatus)DMP45 (21)36 (20)40 (22)DEP82 (22)92 (6)87 (15)DIBP83 (28)88 (8)85 (18)DBP92 (20)107 (11)100 (16)BBP114 (31)78 (7)92 (28)DEHP110 (20)83 (3)97 (21)DNOP75 (4)57 (11)65 (17)Level 1: 25В ng/g of w.w; level 2: 150В ng/g of w.w. steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: The method yielded recoveries with relatively high variation. For T. truncatus samples, mean relative recoveries were between 85вҖ“100% for five of the seven PAEs; DMP and DNOP held lower recoveries (40В % and 75В %, respectively). For G. macrorhynchus, mean recoveries ranged between 53В % and 100% (TableВ 4). Matrix-matched calibrations with the internal standard method were calculated for each matrix, obtaining linear regression with fitting R2 > 0.93. Limits of quantifications (LOQ) of the method ranged between 5 and 10В ng/g w.w. (TableВ 5). The matrix effect varied depending on the matrix and the analyte, staying within the Вұ 20В % limits, except for DBP and BBP in G. macrorhynchus (вҲ’33В % and вҲ’27В %, respectively) and DMP, BBP, and DNOP in T. truncatus (+42В %, +37В % and +48В % respectively).

steps_raw.title: Method validation
steps_raw.instruction: Real samples analysis was conducted on portions of biopsies from the two species (G. macrorhynchus, nВ =В 15; T. truncatus, nВ =В 9), longitudinally cut to encompass all the blubber layers. Results on the PAEs concentrations are reported in TableВ 6 and are further discussed in the related research article.Table 6Results of the phthalates (PAEs) analysis of cetacean blubber samples from biopsies of short-finned pilot whales (Globicephala macrorhynchus, nВ =В 15) and common bottlenose dolphins (Tursiops truncatus, nВ =В 9).Table 6Matrix (species)SampleAnalyte (ng/g) wet weightDMPDEPDIBPDBPBBPDEHPDNOPShort-finned pilot whale (Globicephala macrorhynchus)Gma46n.d.66.53В ВұВ 27.47n.d.n.d. 20В %.
